claim: Cross-check confirms the explorer's claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements comprehensive provider-specific configuration with boundary enforcement. All cited evidence is accurate with correct line numbers and summaries. The implementation actively prevents cross-provider drift through explicit normalization logic.

evidence.summary: executionConfig function verified: implements provider-specific normalization for claude, codex, and custom providers. Line 445-461 contains Codex model inheritance prevention and Claude tool profile normalization.
evidence.why: Independent verification of explorer's primary evidence confirms accuracy of provider-specific configuration implementation.

evidence.summary: Codex model inheritance prevention confirmed: 'if (!model && !clean(ai.codex_model) && /\bclaude\b/i.test(clean(ai.model))) ai.model = ""' actively clears inherited Claude models.
evidence.why: Direct verification that cross-provider model leakage is explicitly handled in the code.

evidence.summary: Claude provider normalization confirmed: clears tool restriction flags (settings, claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools), normalizes tool profiles to boundary/research/full, and disables session persistence.
evidence.why: Verification that Claude safety relies on boundary control rather than tool restriction.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot functions verified: buildBoundarySnapshot (97-108) uses git or filesystem walking; diffBoundarySnapshots (110-120) detects changes. Matches explorer description exactly.
evidence.why: Independent confirmation of git-backed and filesystem-based mutation detection.

evidence.summary: Runtime boundary enforcement confirmed: creates before/after snapshots (514, 564), converts changes outside allowed artifact roots into BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers (567-570, 583-588).
evidence.why: Verification that boundary violations are detected and converted to blockers.

evidence.summary: Cross-provider test evidence verified: codex dispatch test (197-231) confirms no Claude model inheritance and no forced read-only; claude dispatch test (233-272) confirms tool restriction flags are cleared.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms the code behavior described in explorer's analysis.

evidence.summary: Tool profile test evidence verified: claude full/research tests (274-378) confirm explicit authorization requirements and normalization; boundary tests (380-451) cover git and non-git mutation detection.
evidence.why: Comprehensive test coverage for tool profile and boundary enforcement.

evidence.summary: Doctrine verification confirmed: lines 44-70 specify evidence agent protocols, scope requirements, and cross-provider model leakage prevention as product requirements.
evidence.why: Documentation evidence matches implementation described by explorer.

evidence.summary: Double-opt-in verification confirmed: runtime requires both executeAgents (473-478) and allowAgentDispatch (479-482) to be true before spawning agents (507-508).
evidence.why: Independent verification of the authorization mechanism.

assumptions: Codex provider sandbox and approval defaults are safe for evidence mode workloads
Claude provider boundary enforcement is sufficient when tool restriction flags are cleared
Custom provider commands respect the boundary contract when invoked with evidence agent prompts
risks: The incomplete Codex model inheritance check at line 445-447 suggests edge cases where cross-provider model normalization might not catch all inheritance scenarios
Tool profile normalization only applies to Claude provider; Codex and custom providers may have different tool capability assumptions not yet documented
Boundary enforcement relies on git availability or filesystem walking accuracy; sparse checkouts or exotic project layouts could reduce detection reliability
missing: Runtime stress testing with real Claude -p agents in current dirty working tree state
Evidence documentation of provider-specific tool capability differences between claude, codex, and custom providers
Test coverage for edge cases in Codex model inheritance when both model and codex_model are undefined

result.notes: Cross-check confirms all explorer evidence is accurate with correct line numbers and summaries. The implementation has comprehensive provider-specific isolation and boundary enforcement. Main remaining gap is runtime stress testing and documentation of provider-specific edge cases. The incomplete Codex model check suggests a minor code quality issue but does not invalidate the overall safety design.
